location.office_time_required: hybrid — combines time in office and time working from home

level_signals: Independently own a substantial work package end-to-end
Lead a cross-functional team without formal authority
Comp range $204,100–$256,700 consistent with Senior IC
Engage senior engineers and researchers as a peer

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Demonstrated ability to independently own a substantial work package and lead a cross-functional team to deliver it. You take outcomes personally and don't wait to be unblocked.
Strong technical depth, ideally in AI / ML, autonomy, robotics or another deep-tech domain, with the credibility to engage senior engineers and researchers as a peer.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. A careful listener, a clear writer, and someone who can motivate and energise a team through ambiguity.
Proven track record in product management, technical program management or a similar role on complex, cutting-edge technical products.
High autonomy, strong judgment and a bias for action. Comfortable making decisions with imperfect information and adjusting as you learn.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Domain experience in autonomous driving, ADAS, automotive or other safety-critical product areas. Useful but not required. We will help the right person ramp up on the domain.
Hands-on background in AI / ML, for example research, applied ML, or shipping ML-powered products.
Background working across global teams (US ↔ UK / EU) in a startup or scale-up environment.

scope.surface: Wayve AI Driver product — a critical work package within the AI driving features roadmap
scope.business_model: B2B — AI software and foundation models sold to automakers for automated driving
primary_metrics_inferred: KPIs and milestones on AI Driver product delivery
core_responsibilities: Independently own a substantial work package end-to-end. Define the problem, set the strategy, sequence the work, and lead the team to ship outcomes that move the AI Driver product forward.
Lead a cross-functional team of engineers, researchers and PMs without formal authority. Give them clarity, remove blockers, and motivate them through hard, ambiguous problems.

Engage deeply with technical detail across AI / ML, autonomy and embodied AI. Debate trade-offs credibly with senior researchers and engineers, and bring structured thinking to complex technical decisions.
Translate R&D progress and customer needs into a clear product roadmap, sequencing capabilities to maximise impact and de-risk delivery.
Communicate crisply across audiences, from deep technical reviews with engineers, to executive updates with leadership, to customer-facing conversations.
Listen actively to engineers, partners and customers, and turn what you hear into better decisions and stronger team alignment.
Set, track and report on clear KPIs and milestones, and establish the operating rhythm the team needs to deliver.

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($204,100–$256,700 base) plus competitive equity
Encourages applications from candidates who don't meet all requirements
Clear ownership scope and product surface described
Explicit acknowledgment they will help the right candidate ramp up on domain knowledge
Inclusive hiring commitments and accommodation offers stated
red_flags: Role title in the JD body is 'Product Manager, AI Driver' but the identity field says 'Product Manager, Driving Features' — mild inconsistency worth confirming
Visa sponsorship status not stated; E-Verify notice referenced for US candidates implying work authorization requirements
Key responsibilities section duplicated verbatim in the JD, suggesting possible formatting/copy-paste error
No explicit years-of-experience requirement stated, making seniority expectations ambiguous

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and ownership expectations)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description — B2B automaker focus)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities, not explicitly listed)
location.visa_sponsorship (not stated; E-Verify link referenced but no explicit sponsorship policy)
ambiguities: Role title in the JD metadata is 'Product Manager, Driving Features' but the JD body consistently refers to the role as 'Product Manager, AI Driver'.

The key responsibilities section appears twice in the JD with slight variations (one says 'research progress', the other says 'R&D progress'; one says 'with researchers', the other 'with engineers'). Both versions captured; the second version used in scope.core_responsibilities as it appears more precise.
Visa sponsorship policy is not explicitly stated; a US E-Verify notice is linked but this does not confirm or deny sponsorship availability.
